binaries.title: condrv.sys - Use-After-Free via early ObfDereferenceObject in console connection handler

changed_functions.name: CdCreateDefaultObjectClient (sub_1C000A170)
key_changes: CRITICAL: ObfDereferenceObject(Object) moved from early position (0x1c000a274, immediately after lock release) to late position (end of each code path, after all uses of derived pointers rdi_1 and rsi/r15). In the unpatched version, Object is dereferenced before rdi_1 is used to allocate a connection structure, before PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20)) is called, and before rax_5[1]=rdi_1 and rdi_1[3]+=1 are executed.
The fast-path type check reads the same CdServerType global descriptor in both builds (its raw address moves with the build's rodata layout, not a semantic change).

Allocation helper call shifted from CdpAllocateClient (0x1c000a320) to CdpAllocateClient (0x1c000a3b0) (same function body; every function after CdCreateDefaultObjectClient slid because that function grew ~0x90 bytes to hold the added exit-path derefs).
Register allocation reorganized (r15→r13, r14→rsi, esi→r15, etc.) to accommodate the reordered control flow.
changed_functions.security_notes: Use-After-Free (CWE-416). In the unpatched version, ObfDereferenceObject(Object) is called at 0x1c000a274 immediately after ExReleasePushLockSharedEx/KeLeaveCriticalRegion. This drops Object's reference count. If a concurrent handle close from another thread has already dropped the remaining references, Object's refcount reaches zero and the object (and its FsContext2 connection data) is freed. The code then continues to use rdi_1 (a server connection structure derived from *(Object+0x18)+0x18) at multiple points: (1) PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20)) at 0x1c000a2ac dereferences rsi which lives in Object's freed context, (2) rax_5[1]=rdi_1 at 0x1c000ba75 writes a dangling pointer into a newly allocated structure, (3) rdi_1[3]+=1 (lock inc) at 0x1c000ba79 performs an atomic increment on freed pool memory. The patch moves ObfDereferenceObject(Object) to the end of all code paths, ensuring rdi_1 and rsi remain valid throughout.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (vulnerable) key sequence:
0x1c000a265: call KeLeaveCriticalRegion
0x1c000a274: call ObfDereferenceObject(Object)  ; *** EARLY DEREF - drops refcount ***
...
0x1c000a2a9: test rsi, rsi                      ; rsi derived from Object, may be freed
0x1c000a2b2: call PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20)) ; UAF: dereferences freed memory
0x1c000ba75: mov [rbx+0x8], rdi                  ; UAF: stores dangling rdi_1 pointer
0x1c000ba79: lock inc qword [rdi+0x18]           ; UAF: increments refcount on freed pool

PATCHED (fixed) key sequence:
0x1c000a28b: (after KeLeaveCriticalRegion at 0x1c000a27f - NO ObfDereferenceObject here)
0x1c000a2f7: PsIsSystemProcess(*(r15+0x20))      ; r15 still valid
0x1c000a30e: rax_5[1] = rdi_1                     ; rdi_1 still valid
0x1c000a312: rdi_1[3] += 1                        ; rdi_1 still valid
0x1c000a33a: ObfDereferenceObject(Object)         ; *** MOVED TO END - after all uses ***

call_chain: CdCreateDefaultObjectClient has no direct caller in the disassembly; it is reached through an indirect (function-pointer) call from the connection dispatch. Signature: CdCreateDefaultObjectClient(PIRP Irp, int subcode)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Use-After-Free (CWE-416) due to premature object reference release
security_findings.description: The function CdCreateDefaultObjectClient (sub_1C000A170) handles a console connection establishment or server I/O completion IOCTL in condrv.sys. It obtains a reference to a console file object (Object) either from a cached pointer in the IRP stack location (fast path) or via CdGetProcessConnection (sub_1c00098b0) which calls ObReferenceObjectByHandle+ObfReferenceObject (slow path). From Object it derives two critical pointers: rsi = connection data at *(Object+0x18), and rdi_1 = server connection structure at *(rsi+0x18). After acquiring a shared push lock on rdi_1+0x28 to read rdi_1[0xC0] (Object_1) and referencing Object_1, the lock is released. Then, at 0x1c000a274, ObfDereferenceObject(Object) is called, dropping Object's reference count. If a concurrent handle close on another thread has already removed the other references to Object, this dereference brings the refcount to zero, triggering the object's cleanup routine which frees Object and its associated FsContext2 connection data (rsi). The freed structures are subsequently used: PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20)) dereferences freed pool, rdi_1 is stored into a newly allocated structure, and an atomic increment (lock inc [rdi_1+0x18]) operates on freed memory. This creates exploitable use-after-free conditions.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Open a console connection handle to \Device\ConDrv. 2. Issue the IOCTL that dispatches to CdCreateDefaultObjectClient (sub_1C000A170) (connection establishment/server connection). This must go through the slow path (CdGetProcessConnection (sub_1c00098b0)) or fast path where Object is referenced. 3. From a second thread, close the same console handle concurrently, racing to drop Object's reference count to zero. 4. The race window is between 0x1c000a274 (ObfDereferenceObject) and 0x1c000a2ac/0x1c000ba75/0x1c000ba79 (subsequent uses of rsi/rdi_1). If the close fires in this window, the pool backing Object and its FsContext2 is freed before the UAF accesses occur. The attacker must win the race between the ObfDereferenceObject and the lock-inc instruction (~20 instructions window).
security_findings.primitive: Kernel pool use-after-free. The reachable unprivileged access on the slow path is the freed-pool read mov rcx,[rsi+0x20] fed to PsIsSystemProcess. The two write-side accesses (lock inc [rdi_1+0x18] and the store mov [rax_5+0x8],rdi_1) are reached only inside the block gated by PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20))!=0, i.e. only when the connection's owning process is the System process, which is not attacker-controlled for an ordinary connection. As a result the write-side is not a general unprivileged primitive; the demonstrable reachable effect of the race is a freed-pool access whose most likely outcome is a crash. Promotion to arbitrary read/write or privilege escalation is not demonstrable from these binaries and is not claimed.
security_findings.assembly:
CdCreateDefaultObjectClient (sub_1C000A170) unpatched full assembly (135 instructions):
0x1c000a170: mov [rsp+0x10], rbx
0x1c000a175: push rbp
0x1c000a176: push rsi
0x1c000a177: push rdi
0x1c000a178: push r12
0x1c000a17a: push r13
0x1c000a17c: push r14
0x1c000a17e: push r15
0x1c000a180: sub rsp, 0x20
0x1c000a184: mov r15, [rcx+0xb8]           ; r15 = IRP stack loc (Overlay)
0x1c000a18b: xor esi, esi                  ; rsi = 0 (connection data)
0x1c000a18d: mov [rsp+0x60], rsi           ; arg_8 = 0
0x1c000a192: mov r13d, edx                 ; r13d = arg2 (IOCTL subcode)
0x1c000a195: mov r14, rcx                  ; r14 = IRP
0x1c000a198: call 0x1c0009be0              ; get process (CdpGetRequestorProcess (sub_1c0009be0))
0x1c000a19d: mov r9, rax
0x1c000a1a0: test rax, rax
0x1c000a1a3: je 0x1c000ba2e                ; if no process -> STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_REQUEST
0x1c000ba2e: mov ebx, 0xc000000b
0x1c000ba33: jmp 0x1c000ba88
0x1c000a1a9: mov rax, [r15+0x8]            ; rax = *(Overlay+8) = IO_STACK params
0x1c000a1ad: test dword [rax+0x10], 0xffedfe40  ; access mask check
0x1c000a1b4: jne 0x1c000ba35               ; if bad access -> STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED
0x1c000ba35: mov ebx, 0xc0000022
0x1c000ba3a: jmp 0x1c000ba88
0x1c000a1ba: mov rax, [r15+0x30]           ; rax = *(Overlay+0x30) = file object context
0x1c000a1be: mov rbx, [rax+0x40]           ; rbx = Object = cached file object
0x1c000a1c2: mov [rsp+0x70], rbx           ; Object_2 = Object
0x1c000a1c7: test rbx, rbx
0x1c000a1ca: je 0x1c000a1f2                ; if no cached object -> slow path
0x1c000a1cc: mov rax, [rel 0x1c0004140]    ; CdDeviceObject (data_1c0004140) = ConDrv type descriptor
0x1c000a1d3: cmp [rbx+0x8], rax            ; type check: is Object a ConDrv object?
0x1c000a1d7: jne 0x1c000a1f2               ; if wrong type -> slow path
0x1c000a1d9: mov rdi, [rbx+0x18]           ; rdi_1 = *(Object+0x18) = FsContext2
0x1c000a1dd: test rdi, rdi
0x1c000a1e0: je 0x1c000a1f2                ; if null -> slow path
0x1c000a1e2: lea rax, [rel 0x1c0003180]    ; type vtable descriptor
0x1c000a1e9: cmp [rdi], rax                ; type check on FsContext2
0x1c000a1ec: je 0x1c000ba3c                ; if matches -> fast path (ObfReferenceObject)
0x1c000ba3c: mov rcx, rbx
0x1c000ba3f: call ObfReferenceObject        ; +1 ref on Object (fast path)
0x1c000ba4c: jmp 0x1c000a21a               ; goto lock section
;
; SLOW PATH: CdGetProcessConnection (sub_1c00098b0) - ObReferenceObjectByHandle + ObfReferenceObject
0x1c000a1f2: mov r8, r14                   ; r8 = IRP
0x1c000a1f5: lea rdx, [rsp+0x60]           ; &arg_8 (connection data out)
0x1c000a1fa: lea rcx, [rsp+0x70]           ; &Object_2 (file object out)
0x1c000a1ff: call 0x1c00098b0              ; lookup connection by handle
0x1c000a204: test eax, eax
0x1c000a206: js 0x1c000ba83                ; if failed -> STATUS_INVALID_HANDLE
0x1c000ba83: mov ebx, 0xc0000008
;
; After slow path: load connection data
0x1c000a20c: mov rsi, [rsp+0x60]           ; rsi = connection data (from CdGetProcessConnection (sub_1c00098b0))
0x1c000a211: mov rbx, [rsp+0x70]           ; rbx = Object (file object, +1 ref'd)
0x1c000a216: mov rdi, [rsi+0x18]           ; rdi_1 = *(rsi+0x18) = server connection
;
; LOCK SECTION (shared lock on rdi_1)
0x1c000a21a: call KeEnterCriticalRegion
0x1c000a226: xor edx, edx
0x1c000a228: lea rcx, [rdi+0x28]            ; &rdi_1[5] = push lock
0x1c000a22c: call ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx
0x1c000a238: mov rbp, [rdi+0xc0]           ; Object_1 = rdi_1[0xC0] = server object
0x1c000a23f: test rbp, rbp
0x1c000a242: je 0x1c000a253                 ; if null, skip ref
0x1c000a244: mov rcx, rbp
0x1c000a247: call ObfReferenceObject        ; +1 ref on Object_1
0x1c000a253: xor edx, edx
0x1c000a255: lea rcx, [rdi+0x28]
0x1c000a259: call ExReleasePushLockSharedEx ; release lock
0x1c000a265: call KeLeaveCriticalRegion
;
; *** VULNERABILITY: ObfDereferenceObject called HERE, before all uses of rsi/rdi_1 ***
0x1c000a271: mov rcx, rbx                  ; rcx = Object
0x1c000a274: call ObfDereferenceObject      ; *** -1 ref on Object ***
;                                          ; *** If refcount->0, Object+FsContext2 freed ***
;                                          ; *** rsi and rdi_1 become dangling pointers ***
0x1c000a280: test rbp, rbp                 ; Object_1 == 0?
0x1c000a283: je 0x1c000ba51                ; -> STATUS_INVALID_DEVICE_STATE
0x1c000ba51: mov ebx, 0xc0000184
0x1c000ba56: jmp 0x1c000ba88
;
; Allocate new connection structure (pool tag 'CdCl', size 0x28)
0x1c000a289: call 0x1c000a320              ; ExAllocatePoolWithTag(PagedPool, 0x28, 'CdCl')
0x1c000a28e: mov rbx, rax                  ; rbx = rax_5 = new connection struct
0x1c000a291: test rax, rax
0x1c000a294: je 0x1c000ba58                ; if alloc failed
0x1c000a29a: xor r12d, r12d
0x1c000a29d: mov [rax+0x10], r13d          ; rax_5[2] = arg2 (IOCTL subcode)
0x1c000a2a1: mov [rax+0x8], r12            ; rax_5[1] = 0
0x1c000a2a5: mov [rax+0x18], rbp           ; rax_5[3] = Object_1
0x1c000a2a9: test rsi, rsi                 ; rsi != 0? (connection data)
0x1c000a2ac: je 0x1c000a2c6                ; *** UAF: rsi may be freed memory ***
0x1c000a2ae: mov rcx, [rsi+0x20]           ; *** UAF: dereferences freed rsi+0x20 ***
0x1c000a2b2: call PsIsSystemProcess
0x1c000a2b9: nop
0x1c000a2be: test al, al
0x1c000a2c0: jne 0x1c000ba6e               ; if system process -> special handling
;
; *** UAF: writes dangling rdi_1 and increments freed pool ***
0x1c000ba6e: mov dword [rbx+0x20], 0x1     ; rax_5[4] = 1
0x1c000ba75: mov [rbx+0x8], rdi            ; rax_5[1] = rdi_1 (DANGLING POINTER)
0x1c000ba79: lock inc qword [rdi+0x18]     ; *** UAF: atomic inc on FREED pool ***
0x1c000ba7e: jmp 0x1c000a2c6
;
; Store new connection struct into file context and complete IRP
0x1c000a2c6: mov rax, [r15+0x30]           ; rax = *(Overlay+0x30) = file context
0x1c000a2ca: xor edx, edx
0x1c000a2cc: mov rcx, r14                   ; rcx = IRP
0x1c000a2cf: mov [rax+0x18], rbx           ; *(ctx+0x18) = rax_5 (new connection)
0x1c000a2d3: mov rax, [r15+0x30]
0x1c000a2d7: mov [rax+0x20], rbp           ; *(ctx+0x20) = Object_1
0x1c000a2db: mov rax, [r15+0x30]
0x1c000a2df: or dword [rax+0x50], 0x10000000  ; set flag
0x1c000a2e6: mov [r14+0x30], r12d          ; IoStatus.Status = 0 (STATUS_SUCCESS)
0x1c000a2ea: mov [r14+0x38], r12           ; IoStatus.Information = 0
0x1c000a2ee: call IofCompleteRequest
0x1c000a2fa: xor eax, eax
0x1c000a2fc: mov rbx, [rsp+0x68]
0x1c000a301: add rsp, 0x20
0x1c000a305: pop r15 / pop r14 / pop r13 / pop r12 / pop rdi / pop rsi / pop rbp
0x1c000a310: ret

breakpoints.why: This is the ObfDereferenceObject(Object) call that prematurely drops Object's reference. Set a breakpoint here. When it hits, RCX holds the Object pointer (file object). After stepping over this call, check if Object has been freed (pool header corruption). Then continue to 0x1c000a2ac and 0x1c000ba79 to observe the UAF accesses on freed memory.

The test rsi, rsi check before PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20)). RSI holds the connection data derived from Object's FsContext2. If Object was freed by the ObfDereferenceObject at 0x1c000a274, RSI is a dangling pointer and the subsequent mov rcx, [rsi+0x20] at 0x1c000a2ae is a UAF read.

The lock inc qword [rdi+0x18] instruction - the most dangerous UAF write. RDI holds rdi_1 (server connection structure at *(rsi+0x18)). If the pool was freed and reclaimed by an attacker-controlled allocation, this atomic increment corrupts a controlled offset. This is the primary exploitation primitive.

watch: RCX at 0x1c000a274 = Object pointer (file object being dereferenced)
RSI = connection data = *(Object+0x18), becomes dangling after ObfDereferenceObject if refcount hits 0
RDI = rdi_1 = *(rsi+0x18) = server connection structure, used at 0x1c000ba75 and 0x1c000ba79 after potential free
RBP = Object_1 = rdi_1[0xC0] = server object, referenced with +1 under lock (safe independently)
RBX at 0x1c000ba6e = newly allocated 0x28-byte connection struct (pool tag 'CdCl') where dangling rdi_1 is stored at [rbx+0x8]
Pool tag 'CdCl' (0x6c436443) for the 0x28-byte PagedPool allocation from CdpAllocateClient (sub_1c000a320)
key_offsets: 0x1c000a274: call ObfDereferenceObject(Object) - THE PREMATURE DEREFERENCE (the vulnerability)
0x1c000a2ae: mov rcx, [rsi+0x20] - UAF read of freed connection data
0x1c000ba75: mov [rbx+0x8], rdi - UAF write storing dangling pointer into new allocation
0x1c000ba79: lock inc qword [rdi+0x18] - UAF atomic increment on freed pool (primary exploit primitive)
0x1c000a21a-0x1c000a265: lock section (KeEnterCriticalRegion + ExAcquirePushLockSharedEx on rdi_1+0x28) - the race window starts after lock release at 0x1c000a265
In patched version, ObfDereferenceObject is at ~0x1c000a33a (end of success path, after all uses)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Open \Device\ConDrv (or equivalent console device) via NtCreateFile to obtain a console connection handle. 2. Establish a console connection that populates the file object's FsContext2 with a server connection structure. 3. From thread A, issue the IOCTL that dispatches to CdCreateDefaultObjectClient (sub_1C000A170) (the connection establishment/server I/O completion handler). This triggers the ObfReferenceObject+ObfDereferenceObject sequence. 4. From thread B, simultaneously call CloseHandle on the same console handle to race the reference count to zero. The goal is for thread B's close to win the race so that when 0x1c000a274 executes ObfDereferenceObject, the refcount drops to 0, freeing Object and its FsContext2 before the freed-pool accesses at 0x1c000a2ac/0x1c000ba75/0x1c000ba79 execute. Note the accesses at 0x1c000ba75/0x1c000ba79 additionally require PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20))!=0 (a System-process connection).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On a winning race the freed-pool access faults; without Driver Verifier the typical result is a 0x50 (PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA) or other pool-corruption bugcheck when reading rsi+0x20 or touching rdi_1+0x18. With Driver Verifier Special Pool the freed-pool access is flagged at the faulting instruction.
debugging_aids.notes: The early ObfDereferenceObject(Object) at 0x1c000a274 executes on both the fast and slow paths, but the subsequent freed-pool USES require rsi!=0, which only holds on the slow path (via CdGetProcessConnection); on the fast path rsi==0 so the test rsi,rsi at 0x1c000a2a9 short-circuits and no derived pointer is used after the deref. The write-side accesses (mov [rbx+8],rdi and lock inc [rdi+0x18]) are additionally gated behind PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20))!=0 (System-process connections only), so they are not a general unprivileged primitive. The fast-path type check reads the same CdServerType descriptor in both builds (raw address moves with rodata layout, not a semantic change). Pool tag for the connection structure is 'CdCl' (0x6c436443), size 0x28 bytes, PagedPool. The lock inc [rdi+0x18] increments a reference-count-like field at offset 0x18 of the server-connection structure.

summary: The patch fixes a Use-After-Free vulnerability (CWE-416) in condrv.sys's console connection handler (CdCreateDefaultObjectClient (sub_1C000A170)). In the unpatched version, ObfDereferenceObject(Object) is called prematurely at 0x1c000a274 (immediately after releasing a shared push lock), before the code continues to use pointers derived from Object (rdi_1 at *(Object+0x18)+0x18 and rsi at *(Object+0x18)). If a concurrent handle close races the reference count to zero, the object and its connection data are freed, causing subsequent freed-pool accesses: PsIsSystemProcess(*(rsi+0x20)) reads freed pool, and (only when that connection is owned by the System process) a dangling rdi_1 pointer is stored into a new allocation and lock inc [rdi_1+0x18] performs an atomic increment on freed memory. The patch moves ObfDereferenceObject(Object) to the end of every code path (success and both error exits), after the last use of the derived pointers. The freed-pool uses occur only on the slow path (rsi!=0, via CdGetProcessConnection). Severity is Medium: the write-side accesses are gated behind a non-attacker-controlled System-process predicate, so the reachable unprivileged effect of winning the race is a freed-pool access whose most likely outcome is a crash; promotion to arbitrary read/write or privilege escalation is not demonstrable from these binaries.
